content.full_text:
City of Torrance

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY  
KENNETH L. NELSON

July 10, 1990

Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel  
California Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
Post Office Box 807  
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

SUBJECT: Request For Advice

As City Attorney for the City of Torrance and Counsel to the Redevelopment Agency, this letter shall serve as a request pursuant to 2 California Administrative Code Section 18329 for advice on the facts contained in this letter.

This request is made concerning Bill Applegate, Council Member of the City of Torrance and Board Member of the Redevelopment Agency. Councilman Applegate is a commercial and industrial real estate broker. In the course of his business he has been, and will be leasing and arranging for leases of commercial office buildings or suites within those buildings in the City of Torrance. We assume that he will be receiving commissions in excess of $250 for those leases, thus making them a source of income for him.

Our City Council recently adopted the City’s annual budget. This action included adoption of an ordinance which would impose a $.05 per square foot tax on commercial office buildings. Following my advice, Councilman Applegate did not participate in this decision regarding the tax increase.

Clearly, at this point we are unable to anticipate all issues that may come before our City Council and Redevelopment Agency. Future action by the City Council will undoubtedly include similar matters to the example described above.

Please advise us on Councilman Applegate’s participation in future decisions of the Torrance City Council and Redevelopment Agency on the basis of the facts set forth in this letter. Please feel free to contact me if any additional information is necessary for your response.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
Kenneth L. Nelson  
City Attorney
